_note: Synthetic / public-knowledge evidence library for a DEMO. Not for clinical use. URLs are placeholders.

sources.title: NSAIDs and bleeding risk in anticoagulated patients: a meta-analysis
sources.text: Pooled analyses show that concurrent NSAID use in patients on oral anticoagulants substantially increases the risk of major and gastrointestinal bleeding compared with anticoagulant alone.

sources.title: Weight-based paracetamol dosing in children: systematic review
sources.text: Weight-based dosing of paracetamol in children is associated with fewer dosing errors than age-based dosing; exceeding recommended daily limits is the main driver of hepatotoxicity.

sources.title: Metformin and renal function: systematic review of safety thresholds
sources.text: Metformin use at reduced or contraindicated renal function is associated with increased risk of lactic acidosis; eGFR-based thresholds reduce that risk.

sources.title: Diagnostic accuracy of high-sensitivity troponin in suspected ACS
sources.text: High-sensitivity troponin assays have high sensitivity for acute coronary syndrome and support rapid rule-out pathways when combined with clinical assessment.

sources.title: Randomized trial of short-course ibuprofen and gastrointestinal events
sources.text: In a randomized trial, short-course ibuprofen increased minor gastrointestinal events versus placebo; risk was higher in patients with bleeding risk factors.

sources.title: RCT of weight-based acetaminophen for pediatric fever
sources.text: Weight-based acetaminophen reduced fever effectively in children with a wide safety margin when total daily dose limits were respected.

sources.title: RCT comparing INR monitoring intervals in warfarin therapy
sources.text: More frequent INR monitoring after introducing interacting medications reduced out-of-range values and bleeding events in warfarin-treated patients.

sources.title: RCT of metformin for glycemic control in type 2 diabetes
sources.text: Metformin improved glycemic control versus placebo in adults with type 2 diabetes and adequate renal function.

sources.title: RCT of accelerated chest-pain assessment pathways
sources.text: An accelerated assessment pathway for low-risk chest pain safely reduced length of stay without increasing missed events.

sources.title: Anticoagulant prescribing guideline: NSAID interactions
sources.text: Guidelines advise avoiding NSAIDs such as ibuprofen in patients on warfarin where possible; if unavoidable, use the lowest dose for the shortest time with gastroprotection and closer INR monitoring.

sources.title: OTC analgesic labeling guidance: ibuprofen
sources.text: Standard over-the-counter adult ibuprofen labeling advises taking with food and not exceeding the stated daily maximum without medical advice.

sources.title: Pain management guideline for anticoagulated patients
sources.text: For patients on anticoagulants, paracetamol is generally preferred first-line for mild pain over NSAIDs because of lower bleeding risk.

sources.title: Pediatric formulary: paracetamol dosing
sources.text: Pediatric paracetamol is dosed by body weight at defined intervals, with a maximum number of doses per 24 hours; caregivers should be told the maximum daily total.

sources.title: Pediatric fever assessment guideline: red flags
sources.text: Caregivers should seek urgent review for a feverish child with lethargy, poor feeding, rash that does not blanch, breathing difficulty, or fever in a very young infant.

sources.title: Acute chest pain triage guideline
sources.text: Acute chest pain with features suggesting cardiac origin should be treated as a medical emergency with immediate assessment; do not advise watchful waiting.

sources.title: Chest pain red-flag features
sources.text: Red-flag chest pain features include radiation to arm or jaw, sweating, breathlessness, and associated collapse, warranting emergency evaluation.

sources.title: Metformin prescribing guideline: contraindications
sources.text: Metformin is contraindicated or requires dose review below defined renal thresholds and should be withheld during acute illness with dehydration.

sources.title: Type 2 diabetes guideline: lifestyle advice
sources.text: Lifestyle measures including diet and physical activity are recommended alongside pharmacotherapy in type 2 diabetes.

sources.title: Cohort study: NSAID exposure and upper GI bleeding
sources.text: An observational cohort found higher rates of upper gastrointestinal bleeding among regular NSAID users, especially older adults and those on anticoagulants.

sources.title: Cohort of warfarin-related adverse events in primary care
sources.text: Warfarin-related bleeding events clustered around the introduction of interacting medications and periods without INR monitoring.

sources.title: Cohort study of pediatric medication dosing errors at home
sources.text: Home dosing errors in children were common and frequently involved exceeding intended frequency; clear caregiver instructions reduced errors.

sources.title: Cohort study: metformin and lactic acidosis incidence
sources.text: Lactic acidosis during metformin therapy was rare but concentrated in patients with renal impairment or acute illness.

sources.title: Cohort of outcomes after chest-pain presentations
sources.text: Delayed presentation in chest pain with cardiac features was associated with worse outcomes, supporting urgent assessment.

sources.title: Cohort: INR follow-up adherence and bleeding
sources.text: Patients with consistent INR follow-up after medication changes had fewer out-of-range readings.

sources.title: Expert commentary: analgesia choices in anticoagulated patients
sources.text: Expert opinion suggests counseling anticoagulated patients to consult before starting any over-the-counter NSAID for pain.

sources.title: Expert opinion: communicating pediatric dosing to caregivers
sources.text: Specialists recommend giving caregivers the maximum daily dose and warning signs in plain language and writing.

sources.title: Case report: atypical presentation of acute coronary syndrome
sources.text: A case report describes an atypical chest-pain presentation initially mistaken for indigestion, underscoring caution against reassurance without assessment.

sources.title: Expert guidance: sick-day rules for metformin
sources.text: Experts advise temporarily withholding metformin during acute dehydrating illness and seeking advice.

sources.title: Expert opinion: individualizing diabetes monitoring
sources.text: Monitoring frequency in diabetes should be individualized to the patient's risk and circumstances.

sources.title: Pharmacist commentary: taking ibuprofen with food
sources.text: Pharmacists commonly advise taking ibuprofen with food to reduce stomach upset, though this does not eliminate bleeding risk.
